DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS, RECONSIDER PREVIOUS RULING REGARDING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER, APPOINT A REFEREE, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS -- TIMOTHY O'LEARY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. MARTIN GREENMAN AND JARED EIGERMAN APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT CCSF. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER THE PREVIOUS RULING REGARDING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY PURSUANT TO CCP 1008(A). PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO RELIEF PURSUANT TO CCP 1008(B) AS PLAINTIFF WAS NOT THE MOVING PARTY. NOTWITHSTANDING THE FOREGOING, THE COURT HAS READ AND CONSIDERED THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, THE DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION, AND THE TRANSCRIPTS LODGED WITH THE COURT AND FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MADE THE REQUISITE SHOWING PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1094.5(E) AND THEREFORE DENIES THE MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS. THE MOTION TO APPOINT A REFEREE AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN (COURT REPORTER: JUDITH A. DEALBA, CSR #5709, PHONE: 650/571-1473)
ORDER CONTINUING TRIAL
